processing.reason: SNR is very high (314) indicating low noise, so aggressive smoothing is unnecessary. However, spike noise is detected (cosmic rays), which specifically calls for a median filter. A minimal kernel_size of 3 is chosen to effectively remove isolated spike artifacts while preserving the 307 sharp, densely packed peaks. The ultra-high spectral resolution (0.01 cm⁻¹) means even a small kernel covers only ~0.02 cm⁻¹, ensuring no meaningful spectral features are distorted. Savitzky-Golay would smooth but not effectively target spike noise; median filtering is the gold-standard for impulse/spike removal.
